commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Has God forgotten to be gracious. has he in anger shut up his tender mercies. Augustine of Hippo 10. "Or will God forget to be merciful. In thee, from thee unto another there is no mercy unless God bestow it on thee: and shall God Himself forget mercy. The stream runneth: shall the spring itself be dried up. "Or shall God forget to be merciful: or shall He keep back in anger His mercies. " That is, shall He be so angry, as that He will not have mercy. He will more easily keep back anger than mercy.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Mercies? Turning the waters another way, (Muis; Calmet) or going against his natural inclination. Vincit illum misericordia sua. (St. Jerome)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
